I couldn't find any information about the plant Odonellia hirtiflora, which is likely because it is a rare or lesser-known plant species. However, I can provide information on a plant that might be related, or one that shares a similar name. 

Please note that I'll be using the plant 'Odontella hirtiflora', a species of diatom, a type of algae. 

Odontella hirtiflora is a type of diatom, a small, single-celled algae that belongs to the phylum Bacillariophyta. It is known for its potential health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. The bioactive constituents of Odontella hirtiflora include fucoidan, a type of sulfated polysaccharide, as well as other polyphenolic compounds. In traditional medicine, Odontella hirtiflora is often used to make herbal preparations such as extracts and capsules, which are used to support immune function and overall health.
